danger_signals.visual_form: Guard sprites, boar sprite, Celtic enemy sprites
danger_signals.meaning: Combat engagement imminent; player must fight or evade

danger_signals.visual_form: Chest sprite requiring Chest Key item
danger_signals.meaning: Resource barrier; progression gated by inventory management

danger_signals.visual_form: Menu weight counter; heavy items (Locked Chest: 229 units, Empty Chest: 185 units)
danger_signals.meaning: Encumbrance mechanic creates vulnerability; player mobility restricted

safety_signals.meaning: Home base/friendly territory; associated with hero identity

safety_signals.visual_form: Village environment palette (pal.melee.sherwood), accessible via password
safety_signals.meaning: Safe haven; location of defensive/allied mission
Health recovery point; resource for sustenance

faction_indicators.color_identity: Locksley palette (pal.loxley.dark) suggests darker, forest-associated aesthetic
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Locksley brand (appears on Locksley Sword, Locksley Armor, Locksley Bow, implied Locksley Shield)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Sherwood Village, Sherwood forests, Locksley Castle, outlaws' hideouts

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Sheriff's castle as main stronghold; guard uniformity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Sheriff's castle interior, town gallows, Arab jail (catacombs), tax wagon routes
Celts battle location (password: CELTS); external/foreign threat
Chapel (meeting point with Marian); cathedral (rescue maiden location)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Title screen with regional differences: US vs EU/German border styling, font variations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Password system allows non-linear world access; debug mode reveals all locations as navigable states
Player must escape; progression gated by location

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cathedral/chapel environments become active; Marian character becomes visible/interactable
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Wedding/chapel conclusion triggered; final story beat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gold wagon routes become visible/accessible on map
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat encounters against tax collectors; resource interception gameplay

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sherwood village palette activates; defense structures implied
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Village defense mission; player protects home location

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Exploration state; multiple location palettes become relevant
Castle interior duel palette (pal.duel.castle) activates; confined arena

environmental_storytelling.example: Unique palettes for Sherwood (pal.sherwood), Locksley dark (pal.loxley.dark), Church (pal.melee.church), Castle duel arena (pal.duel.castle), Dubois exterior (pal.duboisout), Hut (pal.hut) communicate distinct narrative spaces and faction control
Locksley-branded equipment set (Locksley Sword, Armor, Bow) represents hero's identity; Shield variants (Locksley Shield) signify upgrade paths tied to locations
Chest Key requirement for Locked Chests establishes inventory puzzle mechanic; weight system forces strategic loadout decisions reflecting desperation/preparedness
Guard sprites (sprite.face.guard.chr), Tuck sprite (sprite.face.tuck.chr), Will sprite (sprite.will.chr), Boar sprite (sprite.bigboar.chr) establish faction/ally/enemy visual hierarchy
Hadrian's Wall, Dubois Manor, Horse race chase, Haunted well, Gold wagon routes, Town gallows create narrative beats tied to geography

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Presence in Sheriff-controlled locations (castle, jail, town); blocks passage; aggresses on proximity
Aggressive; requires specific combat sequence; location: forest/hunting ground
Appears in cathedral/chapel locations; triggers story conclusion; rescue is narrative objective
Duel encounter in castle interior (pal.duel.castle); scripted boss battle; represents authority threat
Appears in outlaw locations; supports player progression; allied sprite
Church-associated sprite; appears in chapel/cathedral contexts; narrative facilitator
Combat encounter; faction-specific enemy set; external invasion narrative

notes: Robin Hood: Prince of Thieves (NES) is a Zelda-like action-adventure using palette-swapping and sprite sets to communicate faction control and narrative progression. The game uses location-specific color schemes (palettes) to establish narrative geography: Sherwood (outlaw territory), Locksley (home base), Castle/Church (authority locations), and Dubois Manor (external mission). Danger is communicated through enemy sprite presence and confined interior palettes (castle duel arena). Safety is signaled via Locksley/Sherwood aesthetics and healing locations. The inventory weight system creates a secondary danger state through encumbrance. Story progression is tied to location access and character encounters rather than explicit world-state visual changes. Password system reveals that all narrative locations are abstract game states rather than a unified world representation.
sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Robin Hood: Prince of Thieves NES article)
